content.full_text:
McDONOUGH, HOLLAND & ALLEN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
ATTORNEYS

1595 BUTTE HOUSE ROAD
YUBA CITY, CALIFORNIA 95993
(916) 674-9761
TIMOTHY P. HAYES

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
555 CAPITOL MALL SUITE 980
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 444-3900
TELECOPIER (916) 444-8134

TELECOPIER (916) 671-0980

ROSEVILLE OFFICE
2260 DOUGLAS BOULEVARD, SUITE 200
ROSEVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95661
(916) 783-1500
TELECOPIER (916) 773-0295

November 27, 1991

BAY AREA OFFICE
1999 HARRISON STREET, SUITE 1300
OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA 94612
(415) 444-7372
TELECOPIER (415) 839-9004

Mr. Scott Hullabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:

Request for Written Advice re Whether Conflict
of Interest Exists Regarding Annexation to City of
Marysville - Council Member, Darlene Hart

Dear Mr. Hullabrin:

The City of Marysville has embarked upon an effort to perhaps annex
approximately 2,700 acres that lie north of the City. The property is currently in
Yuba County and zoned agricultural. If annexed into the City, it is anticipated that a
variety of zoning uses will be implemented such as residential, open space,
commercial, etc.

Enclosed is a map of the City of Marysville (which does not depict the extreme
southern section of the City) and the area which is contemplated to be annexed
would have its southerly boundary as the existing or current northern City limits,
State Highway 20 on the east and Highway 70 on the west and would extend
approximately two miles to the north of the existing City limits.

Because of Marysville's small size, there is obviously a potential for a conflict
of interest pursuant to the Fair Political Practices Act if the City Council acts on
matters relating to the potential annexation. The City is seeking formal written
advice as to whether Darlene Hart, a member of the City Council of the City of
Marysville, would have a conflict of interest in taking actions relating to the
potential annexation. All of the principal residences of the City Council Members,
except for that of Darlene Hart, clearly fall within the exception provided pursuant
to Code of Regulations Section 18703.4 for principal residences of City officials more

Mr. Scott Hullabrin
November 27, 1991
Page 2


than 300 feet from the annexation in those small jurisdictions with a population of
25,000, or less, and covering a geographic area of 10 square miles or less. The
exception considers the effect of the governmental decision, i.e., the annexation, on
such principal residences in small jurisdictions to not be distinguishable from the
effect on the public generally and thus to not be a conflict of interest.

Darlene Hart's principal residence, however, is located within 220 feet of the
proposed area to be annexed and thus technically does not qualify for the exception.
Ms. Hart believes, however, that she does not have a conflict of interest as there
exists a substantial levee along the northerly boundary of the City which will
remain if the annexation occurs. As a result, Ms. Hart believes that the levee will
act as a barrier and division between her property and the annexed property and that
her property will not be impacted and that if there were an effect on her property, it
would not be distinguishable from the effect on the public generally.

Ms. Hart resides at 1233 Nadene Drive, Marysville, which is located in the
northeast corner of the existing City limits on the north side of Nadene, near Chelle
Avenue and Nadene. Although Ms. Hart's principal residence does not fall within
the exception provided by Section 18703.1 because it is within 300 feet of the
potential annexation, as stated above, she may not have a conflict of interest because
of the fact that there currently exists and will still exist, even if the annexation takes
place, a substantial barrier in the form of levees which surround the City of
Marysville and have existed well over 100 years. The levees are 16 feet high and are
80 feet wide at the bottom and 12 feet wide at the top. This imposing levee which
acts as a substantial division and barrier might very well lead to the conclusion that
the proposed annexation will not have any economic impact on Ms. Hart's principal
residence. In addition, this physical division may also cause Ms. Hart's principal
residence to come within Code of Regulations Section 18703 in that the effect on her
principal residence, if any, by the annexation will be substantially the same as the
effect upon a significant segment of the citizens of Marysville and, hence, Ms. Hart
would not have a conflict of interest. There is an existing road shown as 26th Street
which becomes Jack Slough Road which goes up and over the levee, and the initial
plans for the annexation project which are subject to change propose that Cheim
Boulevard, which is shown on the map in the northeast section of the City, will be
extended to go up and over the levee to the north. These roads, however, would
not seem to remove the barrier and division effect that the levee will have on any
economic impact caused by the annexation for the purposes of a conflict of interest.

Mr. Scott Hullabrin
November 27, 1991
Page 3


     If you have any questions, or if you need any further information on this
matter, please do not hesitate to contact me.

     Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        TIMOTHY P. HAYES

TPH:jkt
Encls.
cc:   City Councilmembers (without enclosure)
      Liz Ahart (without enclosure)
      Ben Bramer (without enclosure)

sections.question: Whether Darlene Hart, a member of the City Council of the City of Marysville, would have a conflict of interest in taking actions relating to the potential annexation of approximately 2,700 acres north of the City.
sections.question_synthetic: Does City Council Member Darlene Hart have a conflict of interest under the Fair Political Practices Act regarding a proposed annexation of land to the City of Marysville, given that her principal residence is located within 220 feet of the proposed annexation area?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney representing the City of Marysville to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section from FPPC is present in the document. The document date is November 27, 1991 (not 1997 as shown in header). Document contains detailed factual background and legal argumentation by the requestor but awaits FPPC response.

embedding.summary: The City of Marysville requests written advice from the FPPC regarding whether Council Member Darlene Hart has a conflict of interest in voting on a proposed annexation of approximately 2,700 acres. The requestor argues that although Hart's residence is within 300 feet of the proposed annexation (disqualifying it from the small-jurisdiction exception), a substantial levee barrier between her property and the annexed area may prevent any economic impact and thus eliminate any conflict of interest.
